reasoning: The severe latency on the A1 interface, the highest level in the O-RAN management hierarchy, takes precedence. The failure in A1 communication precedes and causes symptomatic degradation in the Near-RT RIC's ability to manage the O-CU and O-DU, leading to throughput drops and latency spikes on E2 and F1 interfaces.
actionable_steps: Inspect the transport network between the Non-RT RIC and Non-RT RIC for congestion. Verify the health and CPU/Memory utilization of the Non-RT RIC service responsible for transmitting A1 policies to ensure it is not bottlenecking the downlink traffic.
